title: IM-455 modular garrison
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Structure

|builder=*[[Rothana Heavy Engineering/Legends|Rothana Heavy Engineering]]

{{Quote|Their pacification is your security.|Rothana Engineering motto for the IM-455 modular garrison|<ref name="IHB">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>}}
The '''IM-455 modular garrison''', also known as '''The Peacekeeper''',<ref name="IHB" /> was the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire's]] standard base deployed on planets to keep the peace and serve as a home for its troops.<ref name="SWSB">''[[The Star Wars Sourcebook]]''</ref> It was built by [[Rothana Heavy Engineering/Legends|Rothana Heavy Engineering]] as a rewarded manufacturing contract, and its main purpose was to allow an [[Imperial Army/Legends|Imperial Army]] commander to achieve the same results of turning a venerated political/religious structure into the seat of power for the Empire on a world and at the same time allow for optimized defense. A standard Imperial garrison was elevated for observation and angled for defense, and acted as a symbol to rebellious populations that the Empire wasn't going anywhere.<ref name="IHB" />

==Characteristics==
{{Quote|Greetings! As the commander of an IM-455 modular garrison you are in possession of an impregnable fortress that stands as a symbol of Imperial power.|Rothana's official statement about the garrison|<ref name="IHB" />}}
[[File:IM455ModularGarrisonSideView-TSWSourcebook.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Schematic of an IM-455 modular garrison]]
[[Garrison/Legends|Garrison]] bases consisted of eight levels, topped by a sensor and communications tower. Levels one through to five, all identical in size and shape, consisted mainly of the large surface vehicle bays where all the surface vehicles were stored, maintained and deployed from.<ref name="SWSB" /> The vehicle bays were large enough to accommodate [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]].<ref name="IHB" /> They also contained most of the personnel quarters as well as the armory, detention cells and most other key base areas.<ref name="SWSB" /> It also contained a recreation center, which by [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] added a [[scoopball]] court by popular request.<ref name="IHB" />

Level six held the officers' quarters, [[Imperial Starfighter Pilot|TIE fighter pilots']] quarters, command rooms and so on. Level seven consisted entirely of the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]] hangars. Level eight was the deployment chutes and control rooms for the base's TIEs. TIEs were launched, retrieved and manipulated by the base's powerful suite of [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor-beam]] projectors. Garrison bases also had underground sub-levels which contained the generators (including the main reactor<ref name="IHB" />) and main infrastructure.<ref name="SWSB" /> The roof contained the sensor suite tower, which was equipped with a hypercomm array as well as Rothana's patented mix of thermo-infra-ecto multivariate sensors.<ref name="IHB" />

===Models===
Although the basic garrison was a standardized model, several variations existed:

====Shield projector garrisons====
{{Main|IM-8005 shield projector garrison}}
The '''shield projector garrison''' was a model that was utilized for Imperial orbital construction projects, where it projected an uninterrupted energy shield around the orbital site. It had enough defenses to put down planetside uprisings from the conscripted workforce. It includes a command garrison, which was broken down into several smaller structures similar to the IM-445, albeit with an armored bunker to house the generator; a 20-meter-tall landing pad to accommodate docking AT-ATs as well as two to three ''Lambda''-class shuttles; and the [[SLD-26 planetary shield generator/Legends|SLD-26 planetary shield generator]].

====Oceanic garrisons====
{{Main|IM-A-761 Oceanic Garrison}}
An aquatic model, the '''oceanic garrison''', lacked the underground levels and had several powerful [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlifts]] to keep it afloat. In addition, it was equipped with underwater hangar bays. The contingent included [[Aquatic assault stormtrooper|seatroopers]] and [[Aquatic Terrain Armored Transport|AT-AT swimmers]].<ref name="IG">{{JournalCite|12|Imperial Garrisons}}</ref>

====Orbiting garrisons====
{{Main|IM-S-653 Orbital Garrison}}
An '''orbiting garrison''' was two standard garrisons locked together base-to-base, but this variation was rarely deployed. These garrisons were only deployed in [[Orbit/Legends|orbits]] around extremely hostile and dangerous planets. This provided the troops on the ground with an orbital fire base and also made the headquarters extra secure. Orbiting Garrisons could support a full [[TIE series/Legends|TIE]] [[Fighter wing/Legends|wing]] and several orbital [[Spacetug/Legends|tugships]]. Since the garrisons lacked engines, the tugs were the only means of propulsion.<ref name="IG" />

However, orbiting garrisons created a controversy with the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Navy]], which considered all space-deployed units their jurisdiction. In the end, the command on orbiting garrisons was split between Navy and Army, with one base commanded by an [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]], the other one by a [[Major General]]. Additionally, a new orbital defense platform for the Navy was put into production.<ref name="IG" />

====Hostile-environment garrisons====
{{Main|IM-X-981 Hostile Environment Garrison}}
For planets with unfriendly climate, the '''hostile-environment garrison''' was equipped with sealed airlocks on all entry points on an extra life-support sub-level.<ref name="IG" />

====Garrison complexes====
Several standard garrisons could be deployed in proximity to form a '''garrison complex'''. The buildings would be connected via underground tunnels and walkways. The largest complexes were constructed of up to six garrisons arranged in a ring. Like orbiting garrisons, these complexes were rarely constructed. Only worlds of major strategic importance required such a great headquarters. Most complexes were headquarters of an [[Army/Legends|Army]], a [[Systems Army/Legends|Systems Army]], or a [[Sector Army/Legends|Sector Army]] and commanded by a [[General/Legends|General]].<ref name="IG" />

===Personnel===
[[File:IM455ModularGarrisonTopView-TSWSourcebook.jpg|thumb|right|250px|A top view of the Garrison Base]]

Imperial garrisons were commanded by a Major General and housed 3000 base personnel divided as follows:

*300 command personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*500 support/services personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*200 technical personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*200 science personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*100 medical personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*100 [[Imperial gunner/Legends|gunners]]/weapons technicians<ref name="SWSB" />
*80 [[Walker/Legends|walker]] technicians<ref name="SWSB" />
*70 trade mission/diplomatic personnel<ref name="SWSB" />
*60 ground crew technicians<ref name="SWSB" />
*25 controllers<ref name="SWSB" />
*25 sensor technicians<ref name="SWSB" />
*10 [[74-Z speeder bike/Legends|speeder bike]] technicians<ref name="SWSB" />
*50 [[Imperial Intelligence/Legends|Imperial Intelligence]] officers<ref name="SWSB" />

The numbers of specific staff were flexible to the mission's needs.

Documents produced by the opposing Rebel Alliance stated that Imperial garrisons were typically organized around a Corps HQ detachment from the Imperial Army. Rather than a team of 50 Imperial Intelligence officers, there were in fact 50 [[Imperial Security Bureau/Legends|Imperial Security Bureau]] agents, and only a single Imperial Intelligence liaison, known as the "[[Ubiqtorate/Legends|Ubiqtorate]] Man".<ref name="ISB">''[[Imperial Sourcebook]]''</ref> <ref name="IG" />

An introductory briefing on Imperial garrisons for Alliance personnel, authored by Major [[Viran Qol]], also stated the vehicle-maintenance organization differently - rather than a dedicated maintenance element of 80 technicians for the AT-ATs and AT-STs, there were just seventy personnel attached to these vehicles including their combat crews, implying just twenty support personnel; however, they were assisted as required by an additional garrison motor pool whose primary role was the maintenance of the base's other vehicles.<ref name="IG" />

The ground crew, controllers and sensor techs were part of the attached TIE Fighter wing.<ref name="ISB" /> Major Qol's briefing states that this unit was seconded from the Navy, but lists the sensor techs as belonging to the garrison's Army maintenance detail.<ref name="IG" />

Beyond these support personnel, the actual military component was comparatively small.<ref name="IG" /> A standard Corps HQ had a core of 403 officers and men, plus a 144-man support unit from [[CompForce/Legends|CompForce]].<ref name="ISB" /> But the CompForce detachment was not present in all garrison commands.<ref name="IG" /> The additional garrison personnel comprised civilian political, economic and science missions, though as supernumeraries in a military base, they were all required to have basic firearms training.<ref name="ISB" /><ref name="IG" />

On planets with multiple garrisons, only the primary base had a full Corps HQ deployed, and the others, known as "sub-garrisons", were manned by smaller [[Battle group|Battlegroup]] HQs and lacked the civilian personnel and air wing.<ref name="IG" />

The standard garrison also had the Corps HQ allocation of 2,000 droids, including massive [[I2F series manufacturing droid|I2F-series]] automated manufacturing units, which made them largely self-sufficient in materiel. Many of the base's other droids were assigned to support the I2Fs, including many [[MSE-6 series repair droid/Legends|MSE-6s]], but there were also others, such as servant and security units, which performed more general duties around the base. Astromechs, protocol, and labor units could also be found in various roles.<ref name="IG" />

===Combat forces===
*50 walker crews<ref name="SWSB" />
*40 TIE fighter pilots<ref name="SWSB" />
*800 [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]<ref name="SWSB" />
*200 perimeter support troops<ref name="SWSB" />
*150 base security/detention troops<ref name="SWSB" />
*40 [[Scout trooper/Legends|scout troopers]]<ref name="SWSB" />

[[File:Endor_AT-ST_PTA.png|thumb|right|250px|AT-STs patrol the garrison base on Endor]]

The 800-man stormtrooper unit was the largest component of the garrison.<ref name="SWSB" /> The Alliance briefing cited above stated that it was constituted as a battalion, with the scout troopers as a separate platoon. The garrison's AT-ATs and AT-STs were also attached to the stormtrooper component, although their crews and support personnel were not themselves stormtroopers, but experienced Army personnel.<ref name="IG" />

The TIE Pilots, in contrast, were Navy personnel serving under Army command.<ref name="IG" />

The other troops were part of a straightforward Army hierarchy. In keeping with the Corps HQ structure, the perimeter troops comprised six [[platoon/Legends|platoons]], with a theoretical strength of 228 men (192 troopers, 30 NCOs and 6 officers); they were equipped with specially-trained guard animals such as [[Garral/Legends|Garrals]], and were placed directly under the major-general or else his second-in-command, a [[High Colonel/Legends|high colonel]] who doubled as the base's military intelligence officer. Simultaneously, the security detail was structured as a [[line company]], with a combat strength of 152 men (128 troopers, 20 NCOs and 4 subalterns), and commanded by the "headquarters officer", another high colonel who was in charge of base administration and security. Three more officers of this relatively high rank headed the garrison's non-combat elements, and each of the five had two [[Lieutenant Colonel/Legends|lieutenant colonels]] to assist them. These fifteen colonels also formed the senior personnel of the 403-man Corps HQ.<ref name="IG" />

Although in practice a garrison's fighting strength often mustered only a handful of companies, each base was in principle supposed to be assigned an additional force of four [[Battalion/Legends|battalions]] of Imperial Army soldiers beyond its HQ detail and stormtroopers, and had barracks accommodation available for these personnel.<ref name="ISB" /><ref name="IG" /> One Alliance document stated that the force deployed often comprised the stormtrooper battalion, plus two battalions of AT-ATs and one of AT-STs,<ref name="ISB" /> but Major Qol stated that this was an error, as the AT-AT and AT-ST numbers a base could accommodate were well below battalion strength.<ref name="IG" />

Some garrisons acted as command bases for much larger formations, in some cases as many as sixty-two battalions organized into four [[Battle group|battlegroups]]. These were much too large to house inside the base itself, and required separate barracks complexes.<ref name="IG" />

The standard four-battalion deployment was the equivalent of a [[Regiment/Legends|regiment]] and the largest multi-battlegroup commands were the equivalent of a full [[Corps/Legends|corps]], but the forces involved were not normally constituted as unified formations at the level suggested by their total strength, as they were created by combining various smaller units, and lacked the appropriate high-level command elements, being overseen instead by the garrison's own HQ detail. In practice, garrison assignments were often used as convenient places to base detached Imperial Army units which had become dislocated from larger formations.<ref name="IG" />

Sometimes, however, garrisons had [[Assault (CompForce)/Legends|COMPForce Assault]] paramilitaries deployed at regimental strength in place of Army units, particularly for occupation duty.<ref name="IG" />

Secondary sub-garrisons within larger occupation commands could oversee troops up to battlegroup strength, and were particularly prone to having CompForce Assault units deployed in place of regular Imperial Army formations.<ref name="IG" />

===Vehicle complement===
*30 [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and 5 [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE bombers]]<ref name="SWSB" />/36-40 TIE fighters (three squadrons)<ref name="IHB" />/24 standard TIE Fighters, 4 [[TIE/rc starfighter]]s and 12 [[TIE/gt starfighter]]s.<ref name="IG" />
*10 [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-ATs]]<ref name="SWSB" /><ref name="IHB" />
*10 [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-STs]]<ref name="SWSB" /><ref name="IHB" />
*40 [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] and 60 other repulsor vehicles such as [[XP-38 sport landspeeder|XP-38s]]<ref name="SWSB" />/40 speeder bikes for biker scouts and 60 other combat and support vehicles, including some additional speeder bikes for other personnel<ref name="IG" />/50-100 speeder bikes and/or [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeders]]<ref name="IHB" />
*100 miscellaneous vehicles (cargo [[Load-lifter (model)|load-lifters]], construction vehicles, etc.)<ref name="SWSB" />

As noted above, Alliance material stated that the garrison's AT-ATs and AT-STs, although crewed by the Army, were attached to the Stormtrooper battalion, as were forty speeder bikes for the scout troopers. The briefing source stated that the other sixty vehicles under direct Army command typically included other non-combat speeder bikes and speeders, and an armored repulsor limousine for the commanding general, but also an inventory of combat vehicles - a [[PX-4 Mobile Command Base]], five [[LAVr QH-7 Chariot]]s, and additional troop transports, often including at least one wheeled [[Juggernaut (disambiguation)|juggernaut]], and in bases with responsibility for urban areas of any size, usually a single [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|Floating Fortress]]. Where multiple garrisons were deployed as part of the same occupation force, the secondary bases were often able to obtain multiple Floating Fortresses to compensate for their otherwise reduced manpower.<ref name="IG" />

The TIEs were provided by the Navy.<ref name="IG" /> Variations in the size of the air wing reflect the fact that a TIE Bomber required the equivalent of two fighter slots in the launch rack.<ref name="SWSB" />

===Defenses===
Garrison bases were equipped with three heavy twin [[Turbolaser/Legends|turbolaser]] turrets and six heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser]] turrets, as well as tractor beam projectors.<ref name="SWSB" />

The garrison was protected by ramparts up to 10 meters thick, and had powerful deflectors.<ref name="SWSB" /> In addition to being armored and shielded, garrisons also possessed extensive external ground defenses. Firstly, the base was surrounded by a flat, open area, where attackers would be completely exposed to weapons fire from the inner defenses of the base itself.<ref name="IG" /> This area was also guarded by anti-infantry and anti-vehicle minefields, specialized [[Probe droid/Legends|probe droids]], and a unit of AT-STs.<ref name="SWSB" /><ref name="IG" />

The exterior perimeter was formed by a ten-meter high electrified [[death fence]].<ref name="SWSB" /> This was powerful enough to kill a sentient in an instant, or disable a repulsorcraft.<ref name="IG" /> Lower power settings could also be used, depending on the base commander's choice, where it it could simply shock an individual.

Running just behind the fence was an elevated patrol catwalk with guard towers spaced every 100 meters, each equipped with electrobinoculars, floodlights, and alarm systems. To get through the fence and into the base, units had to pass through a limited number of force field gates, each flanked by an additional pair of guard towers. These gates were usually between two and four in number, positioned evenly around the fence's perimeter.<ref name="SWSB" />

The catwalk patrols consisted of stormtroopers, who also controlled the voltage in the "death fence", whereas the access gates were guarded by Army perimeter platoons, who also provided additional ground patrols directly behind the fence.<ref name="IG" />

Some garrison bases were positioned to use the terrain to their advantage, such as setting them into mountainsides or on outcroppings. These tailored their ground defenses accordingly. Garrison bases also possessed air recyclers and advanced environment and filter systems allowing them to be deployed almost anywhere, from hostile worlds to asteroids.<ref name="SWSB" />

==History==
[[File:Garrison_destroyed.jpg|thumb|right|200px|A garrison is attacked by forces of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].]]
[[Boba Fett/Legends|Boba Fett]] once took an [[Mission to Vryssa|assignment]] to capture a criminal, conveniently the brother of a base commander, on the order of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]]. His actions to capture the criminal forced him to destroy most of the base, including two AT-ATs, two AT-STs, and the commanding general. The engagement gave the criminal time to escape.<ref name="NDP">{{JournalCite|14|No Disintegrations, Please}}</ref> As a result of this, although the Imperials denied the rumors of Boba Fett breaching the garrison, they nonetheless developed and distributed a defensive upgrade called XZD-001: FETT COUNTERMEASURES as a result.<ref name="IHB" />

Star Destroyers like the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class]] could carry one of these installations, while an [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|''Executor''-class]] [[Star Dreadnought/Legends|Star Dreadnought]] could carry up to three.<ref name="SWSB" /> Prefabricated bases were dropped from orbit in ready-made pieces and assembled on-site by Imperial engineers and construction droids. The [[Y-85 Titan dropship/Legends|Y-85 Titan dropship]] was essential to this purpose onboard Imperial ships larger than Star Destroyers.<ref name="ITW:OT">''[[Inside the Worlds of Star Wars Trilogy]]''</ref>

[[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] claimed that he and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] infiltrated the garrisons more than once, and even offered to aid fellow New Republic general [[Crix Madine/Legends|Crix Madine]] in training various troops on how to do so. Madine, however, was skeptical of this claim, even telling Solo that he would have borrowed a [[holothriller]] if he wanted fiction in response.<ref name="IHB" />
